column1: # grep-tells.tsv — TIER 1 (flat-presence) rules for audit-swiftui-drawing-canvas (draw-01 … draw-12).
# severity: hard | warn | adv   (hard → runner exits 2). The runner greps each ERE over the target *.swift.
# This LOCATES only — the LLM agent READS each hit (SKILL.md step 3) and decides. Structural rules that
# grep cannot express (MeshGradient-not-gated, GeometryReader-arranges-children, the Timer-redraw loop)
# live in ast-grep/*.yml (tier 2). Full ❌→✅ + sources: ../references/*.md (routed from SKILL.md).

column4: MeshGradient (iOS 18.0+) — REPORT only if deployment target < iOS 18 AND it is ungated (read in ORIENT); else ignore. VERIFY it is gated #available(iOS 18, *). (ast-grep draw-05 proves the gate is absent)
macOS-arm gate — if it guards MeshGradient in an iOS target, gate on #available(iOS 18, *) (wrong-arm, draw-12, fix_mode: auto). READ the block body. (ast-grep draw-12 proves the scope)

a Timer driving a redraw — if it mutates @State only to re-render a drawing, replace with TimelineView(.animation/.periodic) { context in … } (draw-02). (ast-grep draw-02 proves the @State-mutating loop)

GeometryReader — if it wraps children to ARRANGE/position them, that's layout → Layout protocol/containerRelativeFrame (draw-03, cross_ref layout-and-tables). If it only READS a size into Canvas/Path math, it's fine. (ast-grep draw-03 proves children-arrangement)
MeshGradient with literal width/height — confirm points.count == colors.count == width*height (mesh arity, draw-06). A mismatch is a runtime no-op/error. UNVERIFIED: assert nothing, flag.

a drawing primitive view — if MANY siblings each paint one static shape/glyph, one Canvas { ctx, size in … } draws them in a single pass (draw-01). READ: is each element interactive/identified (keep view) or bulk static paint (→ Canvas)?

absolute hard-coded frame/position — for a RESIZABLE drawing surface on iPhone/iPad this won't survive rotation or size-class changes; prefer containerRelativeFrame / GeometryReader-fed sizing (draw-04). READ: is this a fixed-size icon (fine) or a stretchable canvas (wrong)?
Path drawing commands — confirm subpaths are balanced and closeSubpath() is called where a fill is intended; flag magic-number control points (draw-07). READ the whole Path builder.

hand-rolled Path/Shape — if it draws a plain circle/ellipse/rounded-rect/capsule, a built-in shape (Circle/Ellipse/RoundedRectangle/Capsule) is clearer and correct (draw-08). READ: is the path genuinely custom?

expensive static vector art — if a large/complex static drawing has NO .drawingGroup(), it re-rasterizes each frame; add .drawingGroup() to flatten into one Metal layer (draw-09, cross_ref view-performance). READ: is it static (add) or animated (don't)?

a .drawingGroup() — confirm it wraps an EXPENSIVE static subtree, NOT a tiny/animated/text view; on text or fast-animating content it flattens and can hurt (draw-10, advisory/UNVERIFIED, cross_ref view-performance). READ the wrapped subtree.

a Canvas / hand-drawn data viz — confirm it carries .accessibilityLabel (and .accessibilityChartDescriptor if it shows data); a Canvas is invisible to VoiceOver by default (draw-11, keep-both cross_ref accessibility).
